commentaries.author: Douay Rheims 1609
commentaries.content:
And (b) Amarias the priest and your Bishop shall be chief in these things, which pertain to God: moreover Zabadias the son of Ismahel, who is the prince in the house of Juda, shall be over those works, which pertain to the Kings office: and you have masters the Levites before you, take courage,and do diligently, and our Lord will be with the good
(b) A most plain distinction of spiritual and temporal authority and offices, not instituted by Josaphat, nor any other king, but by God himself. Deut. 17. Num. 27.
